content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 21, 1990

Dal Bural, Manager
Public and Government Affairs
Texaco USA
1121 L Street, Suite 1000
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-90-702

Dear Mr. Bural:

You have requested advice concerning the lobbying disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because
your letter asks general questions, we consider it to be a request
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed).2/

QUESTIONS

1. What is the definition of "filer"?

2. What is the definition of "responsible officer" for
purposes of determining who must sign the Report of Lobbyist
Employer (Form 635)?

DISCUSSION

1. Section 82026 provides:

"Filer" means the person filing or required to file
any statement or report under this title.

_____________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Bural, Dal
page 2


2.     Section 81004 provides:

     (a)  All reports and statements filed under this

title shall be signed under penalty of perjury and
verified by the filer.  The verification shall
state that the filer has used all reasonable
diligence in its preparation, and that to the best
of his knowledge it is true and complete.
     (b)  A report or statement filed by a committee
which qualifies under subdivision (a) of Section
82013 shall be signed and verified by the
treasurer, and a report or statement filed by any
other person shall be signed and verified by the
filer.  If the filer is an entity other than an
individual, the report or statement shall be signed
and verified by a responsible officer of the entity
or by an attorney or a certified public accountant
acting as agent for the entity.  Every person who
signs and verifies any report or statement required
to be filed under this title which contains
material which he knows to be false is guilty of
perjury.
     (Emphasis added.)

     The term "responsible officer" is not defined in either the
Act or regulations.  With regard to the Report of Lobbyist
Employer (Form 635) it is the lobbyist employer's responsibility
to determine who in its organization has the authority to sign
reports on the lobbyist employer's behalf. The signer of the
report must be able to verify under penalty of perjury that the
lobbyist employer "has used all reasonable diligence in its
preparation, and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and
complete." (Government Code Section 81004(a).)

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        By:  Wayne P. Imberi
                                             Political Reform Consultant

sections.question:
1. What is the definition of "filer"?

2. What is the definition of "responsible officer" for purposes of determining who must sign the Report of Lobbyist Employer (Form 635)?
sections.analysis:
1. Section 82026 provides:

"Filer" means the person filing or required to file any statement or report under this title.

, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c)(3).)

2. Section 81004 provides:

(a) All reports and statements filed under this title shall be signed under penalty of perjury and verified by the filer. The verification shall state that the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and complete.
(b) A report or statement filed by a committee which qualifies under subdivision (a) of Section 82013 shall be signed and verified by the treasurer, and a report or statement filed by any other person shall be signed and verified by the filer. If the filer is an entity other than an individual, the report or statement shall be signed and verified by a responsible officer of the entity or by an attorney or a certified public accountant acting as agent for the entity. Every person who signs and verifies any report or statement required to be filed under this title which contains material which he knows to be false is guilty of perjury.

(Emphasis added.)

The term "responsible officer" is not defined in either the Act or regulations. With regard to the Report of Lobbyist Employer (Form 635) it is the lobbyist employer's responsibility to determine who in its organization has the authority to sign reports on the lobbyist employer's behalf. The signer of the report must be able to verify under penalty of perjury that the lobbyist employer "has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and complete." (Government Code Section 81004(a).)

What is the definition of "filer"? 2. What is the definition of "responsible officer" for purposes of determining who must sign the Report of Lobbyist Employer (Form 635)? DISCUSSION 1. Section 82026 provides: "Filer" means the person filing or required to file any statement or report under this title. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c)(3).) 2. Section 81004 provides: (a) All reports and statements filed under this title shall be signed under penalty of perjury and verified by the filer. The verification shall state that the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and complete. (b) A report or statement filed by a committee which qualifies under subdivision (a) of Section 82013 shall be signed and verified by the treasurer, and a report or statement filed by any other person shall be signed and verified by the filer. If the filer is an entity other than an individual, the report or statement shall be signed and verified by a responsible officer of the entity or by an attorney or a certified public accountant acting as agent for the entity. Every person who signs and verifies any report or statement required to be filed under this title which contains material which he knows to be false is guilty of perjury. (Emphasis added.) The term "responsible officer" is not defined in either the Act or regulations. With regard to the Report of Lobbyist Employer (Form 635) it is the lobbyist employer's responsibility to determine who in its organization has the authority to sign reports on the lobbyist employer's behalf. The signer of the report must be able to verify under penalty of perjury that the lobbyist employer "has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and complete." (Government Code Section 81004(a).) If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Wayne P.
